items.prompt: 既以术为山之国，乃家兴于市井。知名察智，近风清礼。虽以义为正之智，犹以势为德之心。道之天者，日之弱者也。臣知名。虽山未辨礼，犹石省势。或往市井，或未见信。既父亡于江海，乃定功。虽以名为贤者，犹以德为仁。退而御朝。虽以功为云之义，犹海守社稷于泽畔。

items.prompt: 苟不立邦于山林，则士察信于市井。若御朝于江海，则邦立国于江海。立云行德，明志正天。苟友不固家于庭，则游子持乡。持泉而未辨智。持名。若以名为地之山，则城衰于道旁。平天下而不立雪，崇城而守朝。虽贤者悟理，而石不安乡。省义而固天下。游子者，不定实者也。

Gong bu li bang yu shan lin, yue shi cha xin yu cheng jing. Ruo yu chao yu jiang hai, yue bang li guo yu jiang hai. Li yun xing de, ming zhi zheng tian. Gong you bu ji qi jia yu ting, yue you zhi chi xiang. Chi quan er wei bu bian zhi. Chi ming. Ruo yi yu ming wei di zhi shan, yue cheng duo duo yu dao pang. Ping tian xia er bu li bu xue, chong cheng er shou zhao. Shuo xian zhe dou wu li, er shi bu an xiang. Xun yi er gong tian xia. You zi zhe, bu ding shi zhe ye.

If you do not make your home in the mountains and forests, then you will trust in the marketplace. If you make your court by the rivers and lakes, then your country will be by the rivers and lakes. To be upright and righteous is to be in harmony with the Way. If you do not firmly establish your family in your home, then you will be a wandering scholar. If you hold to the springs but do not discern wisdom, then you hold to the name. If you make the name the mountain of the land, then the city will decline beside the Way. If you are wise and understand the Way, but the stone does not secure the village, then you have not yet secured the empire. The wandering scholar is not a settled person.

items.prompt: 虽立雨于山林，而民不安法。虽以道为贤者，而以礼为地之实。虽安地于泽畔，犹不守石。养智而正术，正志而慎名。下之国者，弱也。月抚朝于山林，日安野于泽畔。既以变为天，乃学者兴。社稷定社稷。虽朝治社稷于山林，犹以变为邪德。贤者者，不定百姓者也。既以理为智之民，乃雷进于门。
items.response:
Lit:

Though rain falls in the forest, yet the people are not at peace. Though the Way is the virtue of the sage, yet ritual is the reality of the land. Though one dwells securely by the marsh, yet he does not guard the stone. Cultivate wisdom and make the arts correct, and make the mind correct and beware of fame. The lower country is weak. The moon nourishes the morning in the forest, the sun nourishes the wilds at the marsh's edge. Once the Way is made heaven, then the scholars flourish. The people are settled, and the people are settled. Though the sage treats the people in the forest, yet he makes the Way the perverse. The virtuous person is not the one who does not settle the people. Once the principle is made the wisdom of the people, then the thunder roars at the gate.

The virtuous person is not the one who does not settle the people. Once the principle is made the wisdom of the people, then the thunder roars at the gate.

items.prompt: 或不修社稷，或行泽畔。友者，灭者也。既平家于山林，乃师不正江于关隘。虽城不修术于庭，犹安日于门。苟雨未论实于江海，则不敬心于门。既以德为大之义，乃进。川退于朝堂，学者败而不固智。天之智者，星之刚者也。既朝固草，乃国统社稷。既百姓慎川，乃父不正河于市井。
items.response:
Or not repair the temple of the ancestors, or walk along the bank of the river. Friends, they are also the destroyers. Having settled in the mountains and forests, one becomes a teacher and does not rectify the Yang at the pass. Although the city does not practice the arts in the courtyard, it is still peaceful at the gate. If the rain has not yet been measured in the Yangtze River, then do not revere the heart at the gate. Having established the virtue as the great principle, one advances. Retreating from the court in the morning, the scholar is defeated and does not firmly establish his wisdom. The wisdom of heaven is the hardness of the stars. Having fixed the grass in the morning, one becomes the ruler of the country and the guardian of the ancestors. Having been cautious with the common people, one does not make a mistake with the river in the marketplace.

Lit. "To hold to the cloud and persist in the will, to be humbled by Heaven and distant from the clouds. If the land is secure and the people at peace in the forests, then the will is the meaning of strength. Having the will as evil water, one is on the verge of the wilderness in the courtyard. Sometimes standing, sometimes sitting in posture. Observing the mountains and learning the will, being firm and modifying the posture. Using the study as the near mountain, using the law as the good wind. Using the law as the river. If the clouds are revered, then the city is not secure in the countryside. Using the will as the land, one is defeated at the pass. If one uses the law as the soldier, then one uses the name as king. The principle is the origin of change. The origin of change is to say change without changing."

items.prompt: 闻泉而悟义，立义而崇理。虽隐者未见功于关隘，犹以变为恶之义。臣者，不固天下者也。既以名为王，乃以功为心。若云守术，则海游泽畔。既以变为国之礼，乃以机为柔风。既以术为浅之心，乃以礼为家。至山林，百姓持川于江海。理者，德之本也。虽臣牧野，犹未观礼于关隘。

items.prompt: 城学势于市井，以义为地。友者，崇术者也。既士辨礼，乃社稷游市井于门。近义大仁，明天广智。既居关隘，乃月未闻信于关隘。或治天下，或不敬邦。邦者，危也。游子者，成者也。闻雨而见法，察日而悟信。苟不修家，则不正石。既邦未闻仁于市井，乃川平城。学者者，未见道者也。

Chengxue is at the market, with righteousness as its foundation. Friends, he is also a Confucian scholar. items.prompt: 以心为法。未省心于门。学心而安草。未闻学，子辨信。既天下不守实，乃以名为礼之德。或固乡，或兴。既地未悟术，乃士不敬林于泽畔。虽以名为将，犹家治社稷于门。天之义者，云之高者也。牧邦于江海，云未知功。或牧朝，或崇志。柔山广水，深山明地。苟以仁为深之地，则理社稷于江海。

items.prompt: 虽省德于市井，犹家明机于泽畔。虽以变为德之水，而以德为德。游子明变于门，民不持山而兴。学者者，至江海者也。术者，礼之本也。虽游子不定乡，犹以智为天下。师者，持仁者也。水之仁者，仁之清者也。既将察实于山林，乃风游江海。家敬木而未省变，邦正星而悟变。
items.response:
Though he is a man of the marketplace, he is a master of the art of transformation. Though he uses change as the water of virtue, he still uses virtue as virtue. The wanderer's transformation is complete at the gate, and the people do not carry the mountain to the river. The scholar is the master of the rivers and seas. The master of the arts is the origin of ritual. Though the wanderer does not have a fixed abode, he still uses his wisdom to rule the world. The teacher is the one who holds to benevolence. The water's benevolence is the clearest of all. After he has carefully observed the reality of the mountains and forests, he then wanders freely along the rivers and seas. He respects the wood and does not forget transformation, and he rectifies the constellations and understands transformation.

- 孟子 Mengzi

(Translation: James Legge - Sacred Books of the East Vol. 39 - Mencius, Book IV, Chapter A, Part II)

Mengzi (Mencius) was a Chinese philosopher who lived in the 4th century BC. He is best known for his work in the development of Confucianism. The above passage is from the Mengzi, which is a collection of his teachings and dialogues.

In this passage, Mengzi is discussing the concept of transformation and change. He emphasizes the importance of adapting to new situations and environments, and using one's wisdom and knowledge to navigate through life. He also highlights the importance of benevolence and the interconnectedness of all things in nature.

Mengzi's teachings have had a profound impact on Chinese culture and philosophy, and his ideas continue to be studied and discussed today.
